---
title: "ASE Analysis"
author: "Muhammad"
date: "3/19/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir ="/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASEP_Input")


```
## loading required libraries

```{r ASE Analysis}

library(data.table)
library(readr)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(rtracklayer)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(parallel)
library(ASEP)        # Ensure this package is installed
library(npmlreg)     # Required for model fitting in ASEP
library(ggplot2)     # For plotting later (if needed)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)   # For combining plots
library(Rcpp)



```

### ASE Analysis with ASEreadcount data ###

```{r ASE Analysis}
# Step 1: Load combined count data
input_folder <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/round2/5_readcounter"  # Path to .rc.txt files
files <- list.files(path = input_folder, pattern = "*.rc.txt", full.names = TRUE)

count_tables <- list()

for (file in files) {
  tryCatch({
    # Read in count table
    sample_data <- fread(file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", fill = TRUE)
    
    # Extract sample name from filename
    sample_name <- gsub(".rc.txt", "", basename(file))
    
    # Ensure columns for refCount and altCount are present
    if ("refCount" %in% colnames(sample_data) && "altCount" %in% colnames(sample_data)) {
      sample_data <- sample_data %>%
        mutate(refAllele_col = refCount, altAllele_col = altCount) %>%
        select(contig, position, refAllele_col, altAllele_col)
      
      # Rename columns to reflect the sample name
      colnames(sample_data)[3:4] <- c(paste0(sample_name, "_refAllele"), paste0(sample_name, "_altAllele"))
      
      # Ensure 'position' is an integer column for consistency
      sample_data$position <- as.integer(sample_data$position)
      
      count_tables[[sample_name]] <- sample_data
    } else {
      warning(paste("Columns 'refCount' and 'altCount' not found in file:", file))
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Error reading file:", file))
    message(e)
  })
}

# Now try combining the tables again
# Now try combining the tables again
combined_count_table <- Reduce(function(x, y) {
  merge(x, y, by = c("contig", "position"), all = TRUE)
}, count_tables)


head(combined_count_table)

# Remove any unwanted rows (optional, based on your data)
combined_count_table <- combined_count_table[-1, ]

# Ensure 'id' column is created as chr__pos for proper merging
combined_count_table$id <- paste0(combined_count_table$contig, "__", combined_count_table$position)

# Step 2: Get SNP Data (RSID) from VCF
vcf_file <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/sus_scrofa.vcf.gz"
snp.db <- fread(cmd = paste("zcat", vcf_file, "| grep -v '#' | cut -f 1-3"))

# Match positions from combined count table to the SNP data
pos <- match(combined_count_table$id, paste0(snp.db$V1, "__", snp.db$V2), nomatch = 0)

# Add rsID to combined_count_table where positions are found
combined_count_table[pos > 0, rsID := snp.db$V3[pos]]

# Step 3: Get Gene Annotations from GTF
gtf.table <- fread("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Sus_scrofa.Sscrofa11.1.113.gtf", sep = "\t", header = FALSE)


names(gtf.table) <- c("chr", "source", "type", "start", "end", "score", "strand", "phase", "attributes")

# Function to create gene list from GTF
f_create_gene_list <- function(typE = "gene", gtf.table) {
  gene.table <- gtf.table[type == typE]
  gene.table[, `:=`(
    ensid   = sub('.*gene_id "([^"]+)".*', '\\1', attributes),
    name    = fifelse(grepl('gene_name "', attributes),
                      sub('.*gene_name "([^"]+)".*', '\\1', attributes),
                      NA_character_),
    biotype = fifelse(grepl('gene_biotype "', attributes),
                      sub('.*gene_biotype "([^"]+)".*', '\\1', attributes),
                      NA_character_)
  )]
  
  gene.table[is.na(name), name := ensid]
  gene.table <- gene.table[, .(chr, type, start, end, biotype, name, ensid)]
  
  split(gene.table, gene.table$chr)
}

# Step 4: Find genes within the GTF and filter for protein-coding genes
gene.list <- f_create_gene_list("gene", gtf.table[grep("protein_coding", attributes)])

# Convert `ase.pos.list` to expected format
ase.pos.list <- split(combined_count_table$position, combined_count_table$contig)

unique(combined_count_table$contig)  # Chromosomes in SNP data
unique(gtf.table$chr) 

# Function to find hits in the GFF region
f_hit_in_gff_region <- function(gene.list, ase.pos.list) {
  POS.LIST <- lapply(names(ase.pos.list), function(chr) {
    genes <- gene.list[[chr]]
    ase.pos <- ase.pos.list[[chr]]
    if (is.null(genes)) return(NULL)
    
    ASE <- matrix(ase.pos, nrow = length(ase.pos), ncol = nrow(genes))
    MIN <- matrix(genes$start, nrow = length(ase.pos), ncol = nrow(genes), byrow = TRUE)
    MAX <- matrix(genes$end, nrow = length(ase.pos), ncol = nrow(genes), byrow = TRUE)
    POS <- which(ASE >= MIN & ASE <= MAX, arr.ind = TRUE)
    
    as.data.table(cbind(ase.pos = ase.pos[POS[, 1]], genes[POS[, 2], ]))
  })
  
  POS.LIST <- POS.LIST[lengths(POS.LIST) > 0]
  if (length(POS.LIST) == 0) return(data.table())
  
  GENE.TABLE <- rbindlist(POS.LIST, fill = TRUE)[, .(
    chrpos = sprintf("%s__%d", chr, ase.pos),
    name, ensid, biotype
  )]
  
  do.call(rbind, lapply(split(GENE.TABLE, GENE.TABLE$chrpos), function(X) {
    if (nrow(X) == 1) return(X)
    cbind(chrpos = X$chrpos[1], as.data.table(lapply(X[, -1, with = FALSE], paste, collapse = ",")))
  }))
}

# Annotate SNP positions with gene information
coding_hits <- f_hit_in_gff_region(gene.list, ase.pos.list)

# Merge annotated gene data with combined count table
ANNO.CODING <- merge(
  combined_count_table[, .(contig, position, rsID, id)], 
  coding_hits, 
  by.x = "id", by.y = "chrpos", all.x = TRUE
)

# Reorder to match the original count table
ANNO.CODING <- ANNO.CODING[match(combined_count_table$id, ANNO.CODING$id), ]

# View the annotated results
head(ANNO.CODING)

# Merge the two data frames on 'contig' and 'position'
ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno <- merge(combined_count_table, ANNO.CODING, by = c("contig", "position"), all.x = TRUE)
colnames(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno)

ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno  <- ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno  %>%
  select(id.x, rsID.x, id.y, rsID.y, name, ensid, biotype, everything())

# Remove columns 'id.y' and 'rsID.y'
ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno[, c("id.y", "rsID.y") := NULL]

# View updated dataset
head(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno)


# Rename columns
setnames(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno, old = c("id.x", "rsID.x", "contig"), new = c("SNP", "SNP_id", "chr"))


ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno  <- ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno  %>%
  select(chr, position, SNP, SNP_id, name, ensid, biotype, everything())


# Keep only rows where chr is between 1 and 18
ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno <- ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno[chr %in% 1:18]

colnames(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno)

# Convert to data.table (if not already)
setDT(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno)

# Columns to exclude
exclude_cols <- c("chr", "position", "SNP", "SNP_id", "name", "ensid", "biotype")

# Identify allele columns dynamically
allele_cols <- setdiff(names(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno), exclude_cols)  # Select all except excluded

# Replace NA with 0 only in allele columns
ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno[, (allele_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) fifelse(is.na(x), 0, x)), .SDcols = allele_cols]

# View the updated data
head(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno)

# Save the rearranged data to a CSV file
fwrite(ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno, "/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno.csv") 


# Define your dataset
filtered_data <- ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno  # Replace with your actual data frame name

# Assuming ASE_COUNT_SNP_Gene_anno is a data.table
library(data.table)

# Convert to data.table (if not already)
setDT(filtered_data)


# List of tissue prefixes
tissue_prefixes <- c("B", "L", "M", "T", "O", "K")  # Brain, Liver, Muscle, Testis, Ovary, Kidney

# Iterate over each tissue type
for (prefix in tissue_prefixes) {
    # Get all ref and alt allele columns for the tissue
    ref_cols <- grep(paste0("^", prefix, "\\d+_refAllele$"), names(filtered_data), value = TRUE)
    alt_cols <- grep(paste0("^", prefix, "\\d+_altAllele$"), names(filtered_data), value = TRUE)

    # Apply filtering conditions
    for (i in seq_along(ref_cols)) {
        ref_col <- ref_cols[i]
        alt_col <- alt_cols[i]

        # Update filtered_data using := for in-place modification
        filtered_data[, (ref_col) := fifelse(
            (.SD[[ref_col]] == 0 | .SD[[alt_col]] == 0) | 
            (.SD[[ref_col]] < 5 | .SD[[alt_col]] < 5) | 
            ((.SD[[ref_col]] + .SD[[alt_col]]) < 50) | 
            (.SD[[ref_col]] < 0.01 * (.SD[[ref_col]] + .SD[[alt_col]])), 
            NA, .SD[[ref_col]]
        ), .SDcols = c(ref_col, alt_col)]
        
        filtered_data[, (alt_col) := fifelse(
            (.SD[[ref_col]] == 0 | .SD[[alt_col]] == 0) | 
            (.SD[[ref_col]] < 5 | .SD[[alt_col]] < 5) | 
            ((.SD[[ref_col]] + .SD[[alt_col]]) < 50) | 
            (.SD[[ref_col]] < 0.01 * (.SD[[ref_col]] + .SD[[alt_col]])), 
            NA, .SD[[alt_col]]
        ), .SDcols = c(ref_col, alt_col)]
    }
}

# Remove rows where all allele columns for all tissues are NA
allele_cols <- unlist(lapply(tissue_prefixes, function(prefix) {
    # Get all ref and alt allele columns for the tissue
    ref_cols <- grep(paste0("^", prefix, "\\d+_refAllele$"), names(filtered_data), value = TRUE)
    alt_cols <- grep(paste0("^", prefix, "\\d+_altAllele$"), names(filtered_data), value = TRUE)
    c(ref_cols, alt_cols)  # Combine both ref and alt columns
}))

# Remove rows where all the selected columns are NA
filtered_data <- filtered_data[rowSums(is.na(filtered_data[, allele_cols, with = FALSE])) < length(allele_cols), ]

# View the filtered dataset
head(filtered_data)

filtered_data[filtered_data == ""] <- NA



filtered_data <- fread("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASE_analysis/Output/filtered_data_Conditions.csv", header = TRUE, na.strings = c("", "NA"))

# Replace all empty strings with NA
filtered_data[filtered_data == ""] <- NA

# View the updated dataset
head(filtered_data)


write.table(filtered_data, "/home/muhammad/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/filtered_data.txt", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)


aggregated_data <- filtered__data %>%
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(
    across(
      starts_with("B10_refAllele"):ends_with("T97_altAllele"),
      ~ sum(., na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    count_genes = n()
  ) %>%
  filter(count_genes > 2)  # Keep only SNPs with more than 3 genes


fwrite(aggregated_data, "/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Input/aggregate_Gene_based.csv") 


library(data.table)

# --------------------------
# 1. Clean and Prepare filtered_data
# --------------------------
# Remove rows where the gene name is NA
filtered_data <- filtered_data[!is.na(name)]

# Identify allele count columns (those ending in _refAllele or _altAllele)
allele_cols <- grep("_(ref|alt)Allele$", names(filtered_data), value = TRUE)

# Replace NA values in these allele count columns with 0
filtered_data[, (allele_cols) := lapply(.SD, function(x) {
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  x
}), .SDcols = allele_cols]

# Create a unique SNP id column; here we assume the "SNP" column is already in "chr__position" format
filtered_data[, id := SNP]


# --------------------------
# 2. Create Count Matrices
# --------------------------
# Identify columns ending in "_refAllele" and extract sample names by removing the suffix

ref_cols <- grep("_refAllele$", colnames(filtered_data), value = TRUE)
sample_names <- sub("_refAllele$", "", ref_cols)

# Build the reference and alternate count matrices
ref.table <- as.matrix(filtered_data[, ..ref_cols])
alt_cols <- paste0(sample_names, "_altAllele")
alt.table <- as.matrix(filtered_data[, ..alt_cols])

# Set row names to the unique SNP id
rownames(ref.table) <- filtered_data$id
rownames(alt.table) <- filtered_data$id

# Create the total counts matrix (ref + alt)
tot.table <- ref.table + alt.table

# Reassign column names to match design ids
colnames(ref.table) <- sample_names
colnames(alt.table) <- sample_names
colnames(tot.table) <- sample_names


# --------------------------
# 3.Load Sample Metadata (Design) and Save Count Matrices
# --------------------------

# Adjust the file path as needed.
design <- fread ("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Metadata_Final.csv")
# Assume that design contains a column 'id' with sample names matching those in tot.table.
design <- design[match(design$id, colnames(tot.table))]
stopifnot(all(colnames(tot.table) == design$id))

# Save the count matrices and design into an Rda file
save(ref.table, alt.table, tot.table, design, file = "input-tables-ase.Rda")


# --------------------------
# 4. Prepare the Annotation Table
# --------------------------
# 1. Prepare annotation data and match position to ref.table
ANNO.CODING <- filtered_data[, .(id, name)]  # Assuming filtered_data already exists
# Filter for rows with unambiguous gene names (i.e., no commas and non-missing)
X <- ANNO.CODING[!grepl(",", name) & !is.na(name), .(name, id)]
pos <- match(X$id, rownames(ref.table), nomatch = 0)


# --------------------------
# 5. Create ASEP Input Files for Each Sample
# --------------------------

tmp <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/tmp"

# Ensure the tmp directory exists
if (!dir.exists(tmp)) {
  dir.create(tmp, recursive = TRUE)
}

for (sample in sample_names) {
  out_file <- file.path(tmp, paste0(sample, ".gene.txt"))
  
  # Skip if a static output file exists already
  if (file.exists(paste0(out_file, ".static.gene.pval.tab"))) next
  
  # Build the data table for the sample using the correct column names
  Y <- data.table(
    gene   = X$name[pos > 0],
    snp.id = X$id[pos > 0],
    ref.dp = ref.table[pos, sample],  # Use 'sample' directly for ref.dp
    alt.dp = alt.table[pos, sample]   # Use 'sample' directly for alt.dp
  )
  
  # Compute total depth (ref.dp + alt.dp) but don't keep the 'total' column
  Y[, total := ref.dp + alt.dp]
  
  # Filter SNPs based on thresholds
  Y <- Y[(ref.dp + alt.dp >= 50) & 
         (ref.dp >= 5) & 
         (alt.dp >= 5) & 
         ((ref.dp / total) >= 0.01) & 
         ((alt.dp / total) >= 0.01)]
  
  # Remove the 'total' column before writing to file
  Y[, total := NULL]  # This removes the 'total' column
  
  # Check if column names match what you're expecting before writing
  print("Column names in Y:")
  print(colnames(Y))  # This will show the actual column names
  
  # Write the ASEP input file for the sample (with correct column order)
  write.table(Y, file = out_file, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
}


# Loop through each tissue type
for (tiss in tissue_types) {
  
  # Find all files for the current tissue type (e.g., all files starting with "B" for Brain)
  files <- list.files("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/tmp", pattern = paste0("^", tiss), full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Create file names (without extension and path)
  names(files) <- sub(".gene.txt", "", sub(".*\\/", "", files))
  
  # Only keep files with names longer than 3 characters (to filter out unwanted files)
  files <- files[nchar(names(files)) > 3]
  
  # Read and combine the data from all files for the current tissue type
  XX <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:length(files), function(i) {
    # Read the file
    Y <- fread(files[i])  
    
    # Check the column names to see if they match what we expect
    print(paste("Processing file:", files[i]))
    print("Column names in the file:")
    print(colnames(Y))
    
    # Rename columns to standardize (e.g., "snp.id" to "snp", "ref.dp" to "ref", "alt.dp" to "alt")
    colnames(Y)[which(colnames(Y) == "snp.id")] <- "snp"
    colnames(Y)[which(colnames(Y) == "ref.dp")] <- "ref"
    colnames(Y)[which(colnames(Y) == "alt.dp")] <- "alt"
    
    # Add the tissue name as the 'id' column for each sample
    Y[, id := names(files)[i]]  # The 'id' column is the file name, which represents the sample
    
    # Check that the necessary columns exist after renaming
    if (!all(c("ref", "alt") %in% colnames(Y))) {
      stop("Missing 'ref' or 'alt' columns in file: ", files[i])
    }
    
    # Calculate total depth (ref + alt reads)
    Y[, total := ref + alt]  
    
    # Select relevant columns (gene, id, snp, ref, total)
    Y[, .SD, .SDcols = c("gene", "id", "snp", "ref", "total")]  # Explicitly select the required columns
    
  }))
  
  # Sort the data by SNP
  setorder(XX, "snp")
  
  # Save the combined count matrix as an RDS file for the tissue type
  saveRDS(XX, file = paste0("ASEP-input-", tiss, ".Rds"))
  

}


load("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/input-tables-ase.Rda")

# --------------------------
# Source Custom Functions (ASEP_F.R)
# --------------------------
source("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASEP_F.R")  # Change this to the correct path of your ASEP_F.R script
logit <- "logit"




# --------------------------
# 6. One-Condition ASE Analysis (Within Generation, Tissue-Specific)
# --------------------------

library(data.table)

ASEP_Output <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASEP_Output"

# Get unique tissues and conditions
unique_tissues <- unique(design$Tissue)
conditions <- unique(design$BC)

for(tiss in unique_tissues) {
  for(cond in conditions) {
    output_file <- file.path(ASEP_Output, paste0("ASEP_", tiss, "_", gsub(" ", "_", cond), "_resample1e4_seed0.txt"))
    
    # Skip if result file already exists
    if (file.exists(output_file)) {
      cat("Skipping:", tiss, cond, "as output already exists.\n")
      next
    }
    
    cat("Running ASE analysis for tissue:", tiss, "condition:", cond, "\n")
    
    # Subset design for the tissue and condition
    cond_samples <- design[Tissue == tiss & BC == cond, id]
    
    # Read tissue-specific count matrix
    XX <- readRDS(paste0("/home/fb3/iqbal/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASEP_Input/ASEP-input-", tiss, ".Rds"))
    
    # Assign group labels based on condition
    XX[, group := ifelse(id %in% cond_samples, cond, NA)]
    XX <- na.omit(XX)  # Remove rows not in the condition
    XX[, group := factor(group)]
    
    # Run ASE detection
    set.seed(0)
    res <- ASE_detection2(XX, parallel = TRUE, phased = FALSE, adaptive = TRUE, 
                          n_core = 70, n_resample = 1e4, save_out = TRUE, 
                          name_out = output_file)
    
    cat("Completed ASE analysis for", tiss, cond, "\n")
  }
}







# Open a PDF file to write to
pdf("ASE_gene_mapped_tissue_specific_pig_eQTL.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)

# Create the plot
ggplot(data_tissu, aes(x = Tissue, y = Percent, fill = Group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Percent, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "ASE_gene_mapped_tissue_specific_pig_eQTL", y = "Percentage", x = "Tissue") +
  theme_minimal()

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()



library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(VGAM)
library(stringr)
library(tidyverse)




# 1. Load data -----------------------------------------------------------
ase_counts <- read_csv("/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Input/aggregate_Gene_based.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(matches("_refAllele$|_altAllele$"), ~ na_if(.x, 0)))

meta <- read_csv("/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Input/Metadata_Final.csv",
                 col_select = c(id, BC, Tissue)) %>%
  rename(sample = id, Backcross = BC)

# 2. Prepare ASE long data ------------------------------------------------
ase_long <- ase_counts %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols        = -name,
    names_to    = c("sample", "allele"),
    names_sep   = "_",
    values_to   = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    allele = if_else(str_detect(allele, "^ref"), "ref", "alt")
  ) %>%
  group_by(name, sample) %>%
  summarise(
    ref   = count[allele == "ref"],
    alt   = count[allele == "alt"],
    total = ref + alt,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(total > 0) %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "sample")

# 3. Set up tissues and results container ---------------------------------

input_dir <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Input"
tissues   <- c("Brain", "Liver", "Kidney", "Muscle", "Ovary", "Testis")
res_list  <- list()

# 4. Loop over tissues ----------------------------------------------------
for (t in tissues) {
  # Build full paths to the two gene‐list CSVs:
  gl_path <- file.path(input_dir, paste0(t, "_F1_GL.csv"))
  pi_path <- file.path(input_dir, paste0(t, "_F1_Pi.csv"))
  
  gl_genes <- read_csv(gl_path, col_names = "name")$name
  pi_genes <- read_csv(pi_path, col_names = "name")$name
  genes_to_keep <- union(gl_genes, pi_genes)
  
  # 4b. Subset ASE data to tissue & those genes
  dat_t <- ase_long %>%
    filter(Tissue == t, name %in% genes_to_keep)
  
  # 4c. Set tissue‐specific filtering thresholds
  if (t == "Ovary") {
    minGL <- 5;  minPi <- 5
  } else if (t == "Testis") {
    minGL <- 5;  minPi <- 4
  } else {
    minGL <- 7;  minPi <- 7
  }
  
  # 4d. For each gene, require at least minGL GL‐samples and minPi Pi‐samples
  res_t <- dat_t %>%
    group_by(name) %>%
    filter(
      sum(!is.na(ref) & Backcross == "F1 * GL") >= minGL,
      sum(!is.na(ref) & Backcross == "F1 * Pi") >= minPi
    ) %>%
    group_split()
  
  # 4e. Fit beta‐binomial per gene and collect stats
  res_df <- map_dfr(res_t, function(gdat) {
    gene_id <- gdat$name[1]
    tryCatch({
      gdat$BC <- factor(gdat$Backcross)
      fit <- vglm(cbind(ref, alt) ~ BC, betabinomial, data = gdat)
      coefs <- coef(summary(fit))
      
      # p‐value for the Pi vs. GL effect
      pval <- coefs["BCF1 * Pi", "Pr(>|z|)"]
      
      # mean ref‐ratio in each group
      mean_GL <- mean(gdat$ref[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * GL"] /
                        (gdat$ref[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * GL"] + gdat$alt[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * GL"]),
                      na.rm=TRUE)
      mean_Pi <- mean(gdat$ref[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * Pi"] /
                        (gdat$ref[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * Pi"] + gdat$alt[gdat$Backcross=="F1 * Pi"]),
                      na.rm=TRUE)
      
      log2FC <- log2((mean_GL + 1e-3)/(mean_Pi + 1e-3))
      
      tibble(
        Tissue   = t,
        name     = gene_id,
        mean_GL  = mean_GL,
        mean_Pi  = mean_Pi,
        log2FC   = log2FC,
        pval     = pval
      )
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
  })
  
  # 4f. Multiple‐test correction and flag significant
  res_df <- res_df %>%
    mutate(
      padj      = p.adjust(pval, method = "BH"),
      negLog10P = -log10(padj),
      sig       = padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FC) > log2(1.2)
    )
  
  # 4g. Keep only significant hits (and sort)
  res_list[[t]] <- res_df %>%
    filter(sig) %>%
    arrange(padj)
}

output_dir <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Output"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

# 2. Write out one CSV per tissue
library(purrr)
library(readr)

walk2(
  res_list,                    # list of tibbles
  names(res_list),             # "Brain", "Liver", ...
  ~ write_csv(
    .x,
    file.path(output_dir, paste0(.y, "_Differential_ASE.csv"))
  )
)

# ─────────



# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Setup & libraries
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(grid)
library(patchwork)  # for layout and auto-labels
library(tibble)

results_dir <- "/home/fb3/iqbal/mnt/winpro/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/Diff_ASE_Analysis/Output"

# Tissues to include
heatmap_tissues <- c("Kidney", "Brain", "Ovary", "Liver", "Muscle", "Testis")

# Helper to read mean ratios
read_mat <- function(t) {
  df <- read_csv(file.path(results_dir, paste0(t, "_Differential_ASE.csv")))
  mat <- df %>%
    select(name, mean_GL, mean_Pi) %>%
    column_to_rownames("name") %>%
    as.matrix()
  colnames(mat) <- c("F1_GL", "F1_Pi")
  mat
}

# Generate heatmap plots wrapped for patchwork
heatmap_wrapped <- lapply(heatmap_tissues, function(t) {
  mat <- read_mat(t)
  p <- pheatmap(
    mat,
    color           = heat.colors(50),
    main            = t,
    display_numbers = TRUE,
    number_format   = "%.2f",
    cluster_rows    = FALSE,
    cluster_cols    = FALSE,
    silent          = TRUE,
    fontsize        = 8,
    cellwidth       = 30,
    cellheight      = 25
  )
  wrap_elements(p$gtable)
})

# Combine into 2 columns with A-F labels
combined_plot <- wrap_plots(heatmap_wrapped) +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Mean Ref-Allele Ratios (F1_GL vs F1_Pi)",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")),
    tag_levels = "A"
  )

# Save to PDF
ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "Combined_Heatmaps_AtoF.pdf"), combined_plot,
       width = 14, height = 12)

# Show on screen
print(combined_plot)

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# B) BOXPLOTS: Testis, Muscle, Liver (top 6 genes by |log2FC|)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Ensure ase_long is in your workspace

boxplot_tissues <- c("Kidney","Brain", "Ovary", "Liver", "Muscle", "Testis")

for (t in boxplot_tissues) {
  # 1. Read the differential ASE results
  df <- read_csv(file.path(results_dir, paste0(t, "_Differential_ASE.csv")))
  
  # 2. Get the full list of significant genes
  sig_genes <- df %>% pull(name)
  
  # 3. Extract per-sample ratios for all sig genes
  plot_df <- ase_long %>%
    filter(Tissue == t, name %in% sig_genes) %>%
    mutate(ratio = ref / (ref + alt))
  
  # 4. Draw and save boxplot (one facet per gene)
  pdf(file.path(results_dir, paste0("boxplot_", t, "_all_sig.pdf")),
      width = 12, height = 8)
  print(
    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Backcross, y = ratio, fill = Backcross)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
      facet_wrap(~ name, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +   # adjust ncol as needed
      labs(
        title = paste0("All Significant ASE Genes in ", t),
        x     = "Backcross",
        y     = "Reference‐allele ratio"
      ) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("F1 * GL" = "red", "F1 * Pi" = "blue")) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
        axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none"
      )
  )
  dev.off()
}

library(ASEP)
library(dplyr)

load("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/input-tables-ase.Rda")

ASE_B <- readRDS("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/ASE_Analysis/ASEP_Input/ASEP-input-Brain.Rds")

combined_filtered <- ASE_B %>%
  inner_join(design, by = "id") %>%
  select(gene, id, snp, ref, total, BC)

ASE_B = phasing(combined_filtered , phased=FALSE, n_condition="one")

filtered_KIF5A_GL <- ASE_B  %>%
  filter(gene == "KIF5A", BC == "F1 * GL")


plot_ASE(filtered_KIF5A_GL, phased=FALSE)



filtered_KIF5A_Pi <- ASE_B  %>%
  filter(gene == "KIF5A", BC == "F1 * Pi")

Pi <- plot_ASE(filtered_KIF5A_Pi , phased=FALSE)



if (!requireNamespace("pdftools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pdftools")
if (!requireNamespace("gridExtra", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("gridExtra")

library(pdftools)
library(magick)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2) Helper to read a PDF page into a raster grob via magick
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
pdf_to_grob <- function(path, page = 1, density = 300) {
  # Read the PDF (no GS needed)
  img <- image_read_pdf(path, density = density)
  # Select only the page you want (magick-image list)
  img_page <- img[page]
  # Convert to an R raster
  ras <- as.raster(img_page)
  # Wrap in a grid grob
  rasterGrob(ras, interpolate = TRUE)
}

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3) Read your 4 figures (adjust file names & pages as needed)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
g1 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Testis_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)
g2 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Ovary_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)
g3 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Kidney_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)
g4 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Muscle_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)
g5 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Liver_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)
g6 <- pdf_to_grob("Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/PCA_Brain_within_backcross_group.pdf", page = 1)

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4) Arrange in a 2×2 grid and save
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
out_file <- "Z:/FBNProjectGroups/I3-NGS-noncoding/Muhammad/F2embryo/RNA-Seq_data_DE_Analysis/Output/Plots/combined_figures.pdf"
pdf(out_file, width = 18, height = 12)
grid.arrange(
  g1, g2,
  g3, g4,g5,g6,
  ncol = 3,
  top  = textGrob("Combined Panels", gp = gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold"))
)
dev.off()






# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)

# Step 1: Prepare the data
data <- data.frame(
  Tissue = rep(c("Brain", "Liver", "Muscle", "Kidney", "Testis", "Ovary"), 2),
  Count = c(144, 85, 159, 5, 128, 32, 125, 90, 111, 6, 82, 43),
  Total = c(371, 182, 250, 435, 339, 288, 296, 119, 164, 236, 223, 369),
  Group = rep(c("F1 * GL", "F1 * Pi"), each = 6)
)

# Step 2: Compute percentages and labels correctly
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = round(Count / Total * 100, 1),  # correct percentage
    Label = paste0(Count, "/", Total, "\n(", Percent, "%)")
  )

# Step 3: Add layout values for donut slices
data <- data %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  arrange(Group, Tissue) %>%
  mutate(
    SlicePercent = Count / sum(Count) * 100,
    ymax = cumsum(SlicePercent),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0),
    mid = (ymin + ymax) / 2
  )

# Step 4: Set tissue-specific colors
tissue_colors <- c(
  Liver = "#D55E00", Brain = "#0072B2", Muscle = "#009E73", 
  Kidney = "#F0E", Ovary = "#CC79A7", Testis = "#56B4E9"
)

# Step 5: Create a plotting function
plot_donut <- function(group_data, title, show_legend = FALSE) {
  p <- ggplot(group_data) +
    geom_rect(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, xmin = 3, xmax = 4, fill = Tissue), color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(x = 3.5, y = mid, label = Label), size = 3.5) +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = tissue_colors) +
    xlim(2, 4) +
    theme_void() +
    ggtitle(title)
  
  if (!show_legend) {
    p <- p + theme(legend.position = "none")
  } else {
    p <- p + theme(legend.position = "right")
  }
  return(p)
}

# Step 6: Generate plots
plot_gl <- plot_donut(filter(data, Group == "F1 * GL"), "F1 * GL")
plot_pi <- plot_donut(filter(data, Group == "F1 * Pi"), "F1 * Pi", show_legend = TRUE)

# Step 7: Save to PDF with fixed size
pdf("donut_plots_corrected.pdf", width = 12, height = 6)
grid.arrange(plot_gl, plot_pi, ncol = 2)
dev.off()




```
